title: Don't spill expressions to one-use variables due to long line constraints
body:
Instead, print them on multiple lines.  In the module scope, at least, adding assignments has a simulation cost (each assign is logically tracked separately in the event loop).  In always blocks, this isn't an issue.

But there are two cases we need to worry about:
* Too many parse tokens in a statement for other verilog parsers to deal with (Tool limitation)
* Lines which are getting too long.

The former required introducing temporaries.  The latter requires some newlines and indenting in the printer.
